purpose: Sequential character portrait production manifest. Generate files in filename order; include a magenta outputFile label in each cell to verify order; crop left-to-right and top-to-bottom; name each crop by outputFile.

layout.backgroundRule: one continuous flat green background; no visible cell panels, grid lines, borders, gradients, or separators; only the required magenta filename label may appear as text
layout.visibleSpacingRule: portraits should be scaled down inside each crop cell so there is clear green space between adjacent characters and a clear bottom-left label area

filenameLabelRules.placement: bottom-left corner inside each crop cell
filenameLabelRules.style: no outline, no shadow, no glow, no gradient
filenameLabelRules.separationRule: Label must sit on empty #00FF00 green only and stay fully separated from the character art, clothing, hair, props, shadows, glow, and accessories.
filenameLabelRules.safetyGapRule: Keep a clear empty green safety gap around the label. Keep the character centered and scaled down so the label does not touch the portrait or props.
filenameLabelRules.finalAssetWarning: The filename label is intentionally part of the generated sheet to verify order. Crop outputs will include the label unless a later cleanup pass removes it.

cropNamingRules: Crop by row/column/cropCell from this JSON, not by visual guessing.
Name each cropped file exactly as outputFile.
If the generated sheet resolution differs, scale crop coordinates proportionally from the actual width/height while preserving 3 columns by 4 rows.
If a portrait touches a crop-cell edge or crosses a cell boundary, reject the sheet instead of naming by guess.
Every cell must include a small readable filename label using that image entry outputFile.
The filename label must be pure magenta #FF00FF and placed in the bottom-left corner of its crop cell.
The label must be fully separated from the character art and surrounded by empty green #00FF00 safety space.
If a label is missing, unreadable, wrong, or touches the character/props, reject the sheet instead of cropping it.
sheetPrompt: Create one full medieval fantasy character portrait sprite sheet using this JSON only. Use the exact layout, order, crop cells, imageIds, and outputFiles from the JSON. Use one continuous flat pure green #00FF00 background with no visible grid, no borders, no colored panels, and no separators. Keep every portrait fully inside its invisible crop cell and slightly smaller than the cell for safe cropping. Characters should be beautiful, entertaining, slightly cartoony, profession-specific, and varied, including fantasy ancestries and magical traits when specified. Put a small readable filename label in the bottom-left corner of each cell. The label text must exactly match that entry outputFile, use pure magenta #FF00FF, simple bold readable font, no outline, no shadow, no glow, no gradient. Keep the label fully separated from the character art on empty green #00FF00 with a clear safety gap.
sharedStyle: stylized medieval fantasy merchant RPG portrait art, polished painterly-cartoon PC game UI look, slightly cartoony, beautiful, charming, expressive, entertaining, natural facial acting, clear profession silhouette, varied body shapes, rich costume accents, crop-safe bust portrait, one flat solid pure green #00FF00 background
sheetOrderLock: R1C1 = npc-legacy-148-neutral.png — Bix Candletoe, temple sweeper, neutral, goblin
R1C2 = npc-legacy-149-neutral.png — Hanna Milkbell, stable helper, neutral, human

R2C2 = npc-legacy-152-neutral.png — Cricket Marigold, flower runner, neutral, elf

R3C2 = npc-legacy-155-neutral.png — Porrin Mudthumb, pot washer, neutral, human

R4C1 = npc-legacy-157-neutral.png — Hobb Nettle, goat minder, neutral, human

R4C3 = npc-legacy-159-neutral.png — Oti Pebblepalm, stone counter, neutral, dwarf
